title: SACRAMENTO – A group of California lawmakers announced a package of legislation today to bring necessary reforms and oversight to the Employment Development Department’s (EDD) unem

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Widespread reports indicate that millions of Californians have struggled to access unemployment benefits during the pandemic.",
    "EDD has been plagued by rampant unemployment fraud, particularly from California prisons.",
    "Bureaucratic inefficiencies and antiquated technology contributed to the current issues faced by the EDD.",
    "Multiple audits have concluded that the problems at EDD have persisted for decades.",
    "Governor Gavin Newsom established a strike team that produced actionable recommendations for improving EDD.",
    "EDD was overwhelmed and unprepared for the crisis brought on by the pandemic.",
    "The lack of language access at EDD creates insurmountable barriers for non-English-speaking claimants.",
    "Assembly Bill 402 will establish the Office of the Claimant Advocate within the EDD to protect Californians' rights in seeking benefits.",
    "Assembly Bill 110 mandates that EDD cross-check incarceration records to prevent fraudulent benefit payments.",
    "EDD has been criticized for sending billions in benefits to scammers during the pandemic.",
    "The certification process for claimants to continue receiving benefits can be convoluted and misleading.",
    "Assembly Bill 401 aims to ensure timely language support for all Californians seeking services from the EDD.",
    "Recent legislative audits have identified numerous issues within the EDD that need correction."
  ]
}
```
